figures.caption: Figure 8: Surround-view input for Scene 360 (DriveLM-nuScenes). Implicit variant failure case.
Table 6: Scene 120 – Implicit variant success case. Sequential PPP removes the greenlight/stop contradiction. C = structural contradiction count.
Figure 7: Surround-view input for Scene 120 (DriveLM-nuScenes). Implicit variant success case.

Figure 1: Context passing mechanisms for hierarchical driving VQA. (a) Shared setup: six surround-view cameras and three stage-specific questions. (b) Flat: each stage processes image and question independently. (c) History-chain: prior answers flow as conversational history. (d) Injection-chain: prior answers are prepended as structured text prefixes. (e) Proposed framework: learned context vectors (perception to prediction, prediction to planning) are projected between stages via gated linear modules; an optional skip routes perception context directly to planning.
Figure 3: Effective context injection strength (top) and gate opening σ(gk) (bottom) during training, for three projector transitions.
Table 5: Scene 489 – Explicit variant failure case. Injection-chain introduces a pedestriancrossing contradiction. C = structural contradiction count.
Figure 6: Surround-view input for Scene 489 (DriveLM-nuScenes). Explicit variant failure case.
Figure 5: Surround-view input for Scene 48 (DriveLM-nuScenes). Explicit variant success case.

Table 2: Cross-stage consistency on DriveLM-nuScenes. Explicit: Mini-InternVL2-4B-DA (domain-adapted, inference-only); Implicit: InternVL3-8B+QLoRA (general-purpose). NLI uses a multilingual classifier (mDeBERTa-v3-base-XNLI, 100 languages). ↓/↑: lower/higher is better. NLI columns average both stage transitions. Bootstrap 95% CIs confirm planningstage NLI improvements are statistically significant (Section 5.3).

Figure 4: Success case for Scene 162. Stage outputs (P/Pr/Pl) are verbatim (abbreviated). C: structural contradiction count. Underlines mark contradiction triggers/resolutions. Both flat baselines perceive pedestrians but plan to maintain speed; hierarchical variants resolve the contradiction.

Figure 2: Architecture of the implicit variant. The frozen InternVL3-8B-Instruct backbone carries three stage-specific LoRA adapters. After each stage, a gated context projector extracts the hidden state at the final prompt token and injects a normalized, gated projection into the input embeddings of the next stage. The full three-stage chain executes in a single inference pipeline.
Table 7: Scene 360 – Implicit variant failure case. Sequential PPP skip introduces a pedestriancrossing contradiction. C = structural contradiction count.
Table 8: Summary of qualitative examples. Scene 162 appears in the main paper (Figure 4); Scenes 48, 489, 120, 360 are detailed above. All outputs are in English.
